page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.4xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server instance powered by the Ampere Altra processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, providing predictable performance without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results position the ecs.c6r.4xlarge in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 28612.3 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9534.0. Memory bandwidth is solid, with read-only cached small block performance reaching 1137.11 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 69.0 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1104.0 and a weak bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.6 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is also a bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25% at 2558.55.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture of the Ampere Altra processor delivers a favorable performance-per-watt and resource density profile, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 instances for highly parallelizable tasks. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. This server is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 18.56 GB/sec, Redis database operations, software compilation, and lightweight LLM inference using smaller models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server instance powered by a 16-core Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and operates without local storage or GPU accelerators. Utilizing a KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers dedicated single-threaded cores without hyperthreading. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 9534.0 and a static web serving throughput of 18.56 GB/sec. However, single-core and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, Redis deployments, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance offering dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-threaded performance for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 Ampere Altra physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 1:2 core-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU hardware
Strong multi-threaded performance for software compilation and web serving
Qualitative cost efficiency via energy-efficient ARM architecture
Best suited for Redis, static web serving, and parallelized workloads
